name: azure-ai-transcription-py
description: Azure AI Transcription SDK for Python. Use for real-time and batch speech-to-text transcription with timestamps and diarization.
capabilities: Execute batch transcription jobs for audio files stored in Azure Blob Storage
Perform real-time streaming transcription for live audio feeds
Enable speaker diarization to identify multiple speakers in audio
Generate timestamped transcripts for subtitle and caption creation
Support multiple locales for multilingual transcription
Configure transcription settings for optimized accuracy
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only documentation skill containing only informational content about the Azure AI Transcription SDK. No executable code, scripts, or network calls are present. The skill provides usage examples for batch and real-time transcription. Risk score is 0/100.
valueStatement: Need accurate speech-to-text transcription for audio files or real-time streams. This skill provides Python SDK integration for Azure AI Transcription with batch processing and streaming support.
limitations: Requires valid Azure AI Transcription endpoint and API key
Network connectivity required for transcription API calls
Audio files must be accessible via HTTPS URLs for batch processing

useCases.title: Meeting Transcription
useCases.description: Transcribe recorded meetings or conference calls with speaker identification

useCases.title: Media Content Subtitles
useCases.description: Generate timestamped subtitles for video content from audio tracks

useCases.title: Call Center Analytics
useCases.description: Convert customer call recordings to text for analysis and quality assurance

promptTemplates.title: Basic Batch Transcription
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the Azure AI Transcription skill to transcribe the audio file at URL https://example.com/meeting.wav with diarization enabled.
promptTemplates.scenario: Simple batch transcription of a single audio file

promptTemplates.title: Multi-File Transcription
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the Azure AI Transcription skill to transcribe multiple audio files from these URLs: [list URLs]. Enable diarization and use en-US locale.
promptTemplates.scenario: Processing multiple audio files in batch

promptTemplates.title: Real-time Stream Transcription
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the Azure AI Transcription skill to set up real-time transcription for a live audio stream. Configure for English locale.

promptTemplates.title: Subtitle Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the Azure AI Transcription skill to transcribe video audio and generate SRT subtitle file with timestamps. The video is at https://example.com/presentation.mp4.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating subtitles with timestamps for video content

bestPractices: Enable diarization when transcribing multi-speaker audio to identify each speaker
Use batch transcription for files over 1 minute to optimize costs and reliability
Specify the locale parameter to improve transcription accuracy for known languages
antiPatterns: Using DefaultAzureCredential - this client only supports subscription key authentication
Processing audio without checking transcription job status before retrieving results
Not handling transcription session cleanup for real-time streams

faq.question: What authentication methods are supported?
faq.answer: This SDK supports only subscription key authentication via TRANSCRIPTION_KEY environment variable. DefaultAzureCredential is not supported.

faq.question: Can I transcribe local audio files directly?
faq.answer: For batch transcription, audio files must be accessible via HTTPS URLs. Consider uploading to Azure Blob Storage first. Real-time transcription can accept local audio files.

faq.question: What is speaker diarization?
faq.answer: Diarization identifies and labels different speakers in the audio. Enable with diarization_enabled=True to get speaker labels in the transcription output.

faq.question: How are timestamps provided?
faq.answer: Transcription results include offset timestamps for each word or segment, enabling subtitle generation and synchronized audio-text applications.

faq.question: What audio formats are supported?
faq.answer: Azure AI Transcription supports common audio formats including WAV, MP3, and FLAC. Check Azure documentation for the full list of supported formats.

faq.question: Can I use this for multilingual transcription?
faq.answer: Yes, specify the locale parameter when starting transcription. Supported locales include en-US, en-GB, es-ES, fr-FR, de-DE, and many others.
